output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);

    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addNode(observer);
    graph.addNode(target);
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);

    target->valueChanged();

    return std::make_pair(observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount());
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test thread manager registration and safety check
 *
 * @param enableSafety Whether to enable thread safety
 * @return bool Whether thread safety is enabled after operations
 */
bool test_thread_manager_registration(bool enableSafety) {
    auto& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    thread_manager.registerThread();
    if (enableSafety) {
        thread_manager.enableThreadSafety(true);
    }
    return thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

}  // namespace test_reaction

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test batch operations on observer nodes
 *
 * @return true if all batch operations succeed, false otherwise
 */
bool test_batch_operations() {
    // 1. Create observer nodes
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Create batch ID
    auto batch = graph.createBatch();
    if (!batch) [[unlikely]] return false;
    
    // 4. Register nodes to batch
    if (!graph.registerNodeToActiveBatch(node1, batch->getId())) [[unlikely]] return false;
    if (!graph.registerNodeToActiveBatch(node2, batch->getId())) [[unlikely]] return false;
    
    // 5. Verify nodes are in batch
    if (!graph.isNodeInActiveBatch(node1) || !graph.isNodeInActiveBatch(node2)) [[unlikely]] return false;
    
    // 6. Unregister batch
    if (!graph.closeActiveBatch(batch->getId())) [[unlikely]] return false;
    
    // 7. Verify nodes are no longer in batch
    if (graph.isNodeInActiveBatch(node1) || graph.isNodeInActiveBatch(node2)) [[unlikely]] return false;
    
    // 8. Return success
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

output.reasoning:
<think>Okay, I need to implement the test_transactional_update function using the reaction library. Let me think about the steps required.

First, the function needs to create observer nodes. The user mentioned using TestTransactionalObserver, which inherits from ObserverNode. So I'll create instances of this class. Maybe two nodes: a source and a target. Wait, the initial setup probably involves creating nodes and setting up their relationships.

Then, get the observer graph instance. The ObserverGraph is probably a singleton, so I can get it via ObserverGraph::getInstance().

Next, set up an initial relationship. That would be adding an observer from the source to the target. So source observes target. Then, when the source's value changes, it should notify the target.

After that, verify that the relationship works by triggering a value change on the source and checking if the target's changed flag is set. Also, check the call count.

bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Step 1: Create observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("source");
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("target");

    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 3: Set up initial relationship
    graph.addOneObserver(source, target);

    // Step 4: Notify target and verify
    source->valueChanged();
    if (!target->wasChanged() || target->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false; // Verification failed
    }

    // Step 5: Reset observers
    source->reset();
    target->reset();

    // Step 6: Attempt transactional update that creates cycle
    try {
        // Adding target observes source would create a cycle
        graph.addObserversTransactional({target}, {source});
    } catch (const std::exception&) {
        // Cycle detected, exception thrown. Proceed to verify.
    }

    // Step 8: Notify again
    source->valueChanged();

    // Step 9: Verify original relationship is preserved
    if (!target->wasChanged() || target->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false; // Original relationship not preserved
    }

    // Step 10: Return success
    return true;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        auto observer = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name);
        observer->reset();
        observers.push_back(std::move(observer));
    }
    
    auto graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    }
    
    target->notify();
    
    int totalCalls = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        totalCalls += observer->getCallCount();
        if (observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }
    
    return std::make_pair(totalCalls, allNotified);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                            const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                            const std::string& observer2Name) {
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);
    auto observer1 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer1Name);
    auto observer2 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer2Name);

    ObserverGraph& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addNode(target);
    graph.addNode(observer1);
    graph.addNode(observer2);

    graph.addObserver(target, observer1);
    graph.addObserver(observer1, observer2);

    target->notify();

    int total = target->getCallCount() + observer1->getCallCount() + observer2->getCallCount();
    bool chainStatus = observer1->wasChanged() && observer2->wasChanged();
    
    return std::make_pair(total, chainStatus);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test reactive expression using expr API
 *

int test_reactive_expression(int a, int b) {

    auto varA = reaction::VarExpr::create(a);
    auto varB = reaction::VarExpr::create(b);
    
    // Create reactive expression (a + b) * 2
    auto sumExpr = reaction::AddOpExpr::create(varA, varB);
    auto expr = reaction::MulOpExpr::create(sumExpr, 2);
    
    // Return the evaluated result
    return expr->evaluate();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of PersonRecord methods
PersonRecord::PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age)
    : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

const std::string& PersonRecord::getName() const { return m_name; }
int PersonRecord::getAge() const { return m_age; }

void PersonRecord::setName(const std::string& name) { m_name = name; }
void PersonRecord::setAge(int age) { m_age = age; }

// Implementation of test_reactive_fields
std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    PersonRecord pr(initialName, initialAge);
    auto calc = reaction::make_calc([this](const std::string& name, int age) {
        return "Name: " + name + ", Age: " + std::to_string(age);
    }, pr.m_name, pr.m_age);
    pr.setName(newName);
    pr.setAge(newAge);
    return calc.getPtr()->getValue();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto a = reaction::VarExpr(initialA);
    auto b = reaction::VarExpr(initialB);
    
    // Create variable to track recomputation count
    auto count = reaction::VarExpr(0);
    
    // Create calculation that increments count on change
    auto sumFun = [&a = a, &b = b, &count = count](const reaction::NodePtr& node) {
        int newValue = a->get() + b->get();
        count->set(count->get() + 1);
        return newValue;
    };
    auto sum = reaction::CalcExpr(sumFun);
    
    // Perform non-batch updates
    a->set(nonBatchA);
    b->set(nonBatchB);
    int countNonBatch = count->get();
    
    // Create and execute batch context
    {
        reaction::BatchFunGuard g([this](const reaction::NodePtr& node) {
            // Suppress notifications during batch execution
        });
        a->set(batchA);
        b->set(batchB);
    }
    int countBatch = count->get();
    
    // Return final sum and counts
    return std::make_tuple(sum->get(), countNonBatch, countBatch);
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <sstream>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create reactive variables with names
    reaction::Var<int> varA("varA", valueA);
    reaction::Var<int> varB("varB", valueB);
    
    // Create a calculation depending on varA and varB
    reaction::Calc<int> calcAB("calcAB", 
        [varA = varA.getPtr(), varB = varB.getPtr()]() {
            return *varA + *varB;
        }
    );
    
    // Create an action that triggers when varA or varB changes
    reaction::Action actionChange("actionChange");
    actionChange.addObCb(varA, reaction::ObCbType::CHANGE);
    actionChange.addObCb(varB, reaction::ObCbType::CHANGE);
    
    // Collect names into a combined string
    std::ostringstream oss;
    oss << varA.getName() << ", " 
        << varB.getName() << ", " 
        << calcAB.getName() << ", " 
        << actionChange.getName();
    return oss.str();
}

} // namespace test_reaction

Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_filter_trigger` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variable
2. Create variables to track recomputation count and last processed value
3. Create filter-triggered calculation
4. Apply filter
5. Update sensor with first value
6. Record recompute count after first update
7. Update sensor with second value
8. Record recompute count after second update
9. Get final status
10. Return final status and counts

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test filter trigger with conditional recomputation
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update (expected to be within threshold)
 * @param secondUpdate Second update (expected to exceed threshold)
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL
 * @return std::tuple<std::string, int, int>
 *         {final status string, recompute count after first update, recompute count after second update}
 */
std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

But how to structure this? Let's think. The sensor value is a VarExpr. Then, we have a CalcExpr that takes the sensor value and applies some logic (like comparing to levelThreshold). #include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>
#include <atomic>

namespace test_reaction {

std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    // Create reactive node for sensor
    auto sensorExpr = VarExpr<double>(initialValue);
    Var<double> sensorVar(sensorExpr);

    // Create reactive node for status with filter
    auto statusExpr = CalcExpr<std::string>([levelThreshold = levelThreshold](double val) {
        return val > levelThreshold ? "HIGH" : "NORMAL";
    });
    statusExpr.setTrig(ChangeTrig{diffThreshold});
    Var<std::string> statusVar(statusExpr);

    // Track recompute count
    std::atomic<int> recomputeCount(0);

    // Create a reactive node that increments count when status changes
    auto countExpr = CalcExpr<int>([&recomputeCount = recomputeCount](const std::string& status) {
        recomputeCount++;
        return recomputeCount;
    }, statusVar, ChangeTrig{0});
    Var<int> countVar(countExpr);

    // Update sensor with first value
    sensorVar.set(firstUpdate);
    int countAfterFirst = countVar.get();

    // Update sensor with second value
    sensorVar.set(secondUpdate);
    int countAfterSecond = countVar.get();

    // Get final status
    std::string finalStatus = statusVar.get();

    return std::make_tuple(finalStatus, countAfterFirst, countAfterSecond);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

    Var<int> varCount(countExpr);

    // Update sensor with first value
    varSensor.set(firstUpdate);
    int countAfterFirst = varCount.get();

    // Update sensor with second value
    varSensor.set(secondUpdate);
    int countAfterSecond = varCount.get();

    // Get final status
    std::string finalStatus = varStatus.get();

    return std::make_tuple(finalStatus, countAfterFirst, countAfterSecond);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of FieldOwnerNode::valueChanged
inline void FieldOwnerNode::valueChanged(bool changed = true) noexcept {
    m_notificationCount++;
}

// Implementation of ProfileFields::ProfileFields
inline ProfileFields::ProfileFields(int initialScore, bool initialStatus)
    : m_score(initialScore), m_active(initialStatus) {}

// Implementation of ProfileFields::score
inline reaction::Var<int>& ProfileFields::score() noexcept { return m_score; }

// Implementation of ProfileFields::active
inline reaction::Var<bool>& ProfileFields::active() noexcept { return m_active; }

// Implementation of FieldOwnerNode::getNotificationCount
inline int FieldOwnerNode::getNotificationCount() const noexcept {
    return m_notificationCount;
 }

// Implementation of test_field_binding
inline FieldBindingResult test_field_binding(int initialScore, int scoreDelta) {
    // Step 1: Create ProfileFields
    ProfileFields pf(initialScore, true);

    // Step 2: Create FieldOwnerNode
    FieldOwnerNode ownerNode;

    // Step 3: Get observer graph and register node
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.registerNode(&ownerNode);

    // Step 4: Get field graph
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();

    // Step 5: Bind fields
    fieldGraph.bindField(pf.score(), &ownerNode);
    fieldGraph.bindField(pf.active(), &ownerNode);

    // Step 6: Update score
    pf.score() += scoreDelta;

    // Step 7: Record notifications after score update
    int notificationsAfterScore = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();

    // Step 8: Update active
    pf.active() = !pf.active();

    // Step 9: Record notifications after status update
    int notificationsAfterStatus = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();

    // Step 10: Return result
    return FieldBindingResult{
        .notificationsAfterScoreUpdate = notificationsAfterScore,
        .notificationsAfterStatusUpdate = notificationsAfterStatus
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of RecordingNode::valueChanged and notification counter
inline void RecordingNode::valueChanged(bool changed = true) {
    m_notifications++;
}

inline int RecordingNode::notificationCount() const {
    return m_notifications;
}

// Implementation of the test function
inline RollbackObservation test_observer_state_rollback() {
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Step 1: Create nodes
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    auto node3 = std::make_shared<RecordingNode>();
    
    // Step 3: Register nodes
    graph.registerNode(node1);
    graph.registerNode(node2);
    graph.registerNode(node3);
    
    // Step 4: Initial dependency - node2 observes node1
    graph.addObserver(node1, node2);
    
    // Step 5: Save current state for rollback
    auto rollbackPoint = graph.saveStateForRollback();
    
    // Step 6: Replace dependencies transactionally
    {
        reaction::Transaction tx([this](const reaction::NodePtr& node, const reaction::ObserverList& observers) {
            // Reset current observers of node2
            graph.removeObserversTransactional(node2);
            // Add node3 as observer of node2
            graph.addObserverTransactional(node3, node2);
        });
        tx.commit();
    }
    
    // Step 7: Trigger valueChanged on node3 (second sensor)
    node3->valueChanged();
    
    // Step 8: Record notifications after second binding
    int afterSecond = node2->notificationCount();
    
    // Step 9: Rollback to original state
    graph.rollback(rollbackPoint);
    
    // Step 10: Trigger valueChanged on node1 (first sensor)
    node1->valueChanged();
    
    // Step 11: Record notifications after rollback
    int afterRollback = node2->notificationCount();
    
    return RollbackObservation{
        .notificationsAfterSecondBinding = afterSecond,
        .notificationsAfterRollback = afterRollback
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

struct CreateFactoryResult {
    int initialExpressionValue;
    int updatedExpressionValue;
    int squaredValueAfterUpdate;
};

CreateFactoryResult test_reactive_create_factory(int baseValue, int increment) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto var = reaction::createVar(baseValue);
    
    // Create reactive expression (same as var)
    auto expr = reaction::create([=]() { return var->get(); }, var);
    
    // Create reactive calculation (square of var)
    auto calc = reaction::create([=]() { return var->get() * var->get(); }, var);
    
    // Initial values
    int initialExpr = expr->get();
    
    // Update the variable
    var->set(baseValue + increment);
    
    // Updated values
    int updatedExpr = expr->get();
    int squared = calc->get();
    
    return CreateFactoryResult{
        .initialExpressionValue = initialExpr,
        .updatedExpressionValue = updatedExpr,
        .squaredValueAfterUpdate = squared
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <iostream>
#include <set>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of NamedObserverNode
NamedObserverNode::NamedObserverNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
const std::string& NamedObserverNode::name() const { return m_name; }

// Implementation of test_observer_collection
ObserverCollectionSummary test_observer_collection() {
    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Create nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("source");
    auto direct = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("direct");
    auto transitive = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("transitive");

    // Register nodes into the graph
    graph->registerNode(source);
    graph->registerNode(direct);
    graph->registerNode(transitive);

    // Set up direct observer relationship
    graph->addObserver(source, direct);

    // Set up transitive observer relationship
    graph->addObserver(direct, transitive);

    // Collect observers
    reaction::NodeSet observersSet;
    graph->collectObservers(source, observersSet);

    // Check presence of observers
    bool hasDirect = false;
    bool hasTransitive = false;
    for (const auto& nodeWeak : observersSet) {
        if (auto node = nodeWeak.lock()) {
            if (node->name() == "direct") {
                hasDirect = true;
            } else if (node->name() == "transitive") {
                hasTransitive = true;
            }
        }
    }

    return ObserverCollectionSummary{
        .totalObservers = observersSet.size(),
        .containsDirectObserver = hasDirect,
        .containsTransitiveObserver = hasTransitive
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();

        .safetyBefore = safetyBefore,

        .threadCountAfter = threadCountAfter
    };
}

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>
#include <chrono>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();

    // Register main thread
    tm.registerThread();

    // Get initial state
    bool safetyBefore = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    size_t threadCountBefore = tm.getThreadCount();

    // Create worker thread
    std::thread workerThread([&]() {
        auto& worker_tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
        worker_tm.registerThread();  // Register worker thread
        std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::seconds(1));  // Simulate work
    });

    // Wait for worker thread to finish
    workerThread.join();

    // Get final state
    bool safetyAfter = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    size_t threadCountAfter = tm.getThreadCount();

    return ThreadSafetyStatus{
        .safetyBefore = safetyBefore,
        .safetyAfter = safetyAfter,
        .threadCountBefore = threadCountBefore,
        .threadCountAfter = threadCountAfter
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchExecuteResult {
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch;
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch;
    int finalValue;

BatchExecuteResult test_batch_execute_update(int baseValue, int deltaA, int deltaB) {
    auto graph = std::make_shared<reaction::Graph>();

    // Step 1: Create reactive variables
    auto baseVar = graph->createVar(baseValue);
    auto deltaAVar = graph->createVar(deltaA);
    auto deltaBVar = graph->createVar(deltaB);

    // Step 3: Create calculation
    auto sumCalc = graph->createCalc({baseVar, deltaAVar, deltaBVar}, std::plus<int>());

    // Step 2: Create recompute count variable
    auto recomputeCountVar = graph->createVar(0);

    // Step 4: Record recompute count before batch
    int recomputeBefore = recomputeCountVar->getValue();

    // Step 5: Execute batch updates

    batch.addOp([this](const reaction::NodePtr &node, const reaction::Value &val) {

        auto deltaAVar = node.lock();

    });

        // Update deltaBVar to deltaB + 1
        auto deltaBVar = node.lock();
        if (deltaBVar) {
            deltaBVar->updateValue(deltaBVar->getValue().get<int>() + 1);
        }
    });
    batch.execute();

    // Step 6: Record recompute count after batch
    int recomputeAfter = recomputeCountVar->getValue();

    // Step 7: Get final value
    int finalValue = sumCalc->getValue().get<int>();

    // Step 8: Return result
    return BatchExecuteResult{
        .recomputeCountBeforeBatch = recomputeBefore,
        .recomputeCountAfterBatch = recomputeAfter,
        .finalValue = finalValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>
```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchExecuteResult {
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch;
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch;
    int finalValue;
};

BatchExecuteResult test_batch_execute_update(int baseValue, int deltaA, int deltaB) {
    auto graph = std::make_shared<reaction::Graph>();
    
    // 1. Create reactive variables
    auto baseVar = graph->createVar(baseValue);
    auto deltaAVar = graph->createVar(deltaA);
    auto deltaBVar = graph->createVar(deltaB);
    
    // 3. Create calculation
    auto sumCalc = graph->createCalc({baseVar, deltaAVar, deltaBVar}, std::plus<int>());
    
    // 2. Create recompute count variable
    auto recomputeCountVar = graph->createVar(0);
    
    // Add filter to increment recompute count when sum changes
    sumCalc->addFilter([this](const reaction::Value& oldVal, const reaction::Value& newVal) {
        if (oldVal != newVal) {
            auto count = recomputeCountVar->getValue();
            recomputeCountVar->updateValue(count + 1);
        }
        return true;
    });
    
    // 4. Record recompute count before batch
    int recomputeBefore = recomputeCountVar->getValue();
    
    // 5. Execute batch updates
    reaction::Batch batch;
    batch.addOp([this](const reaction::NodePtr& node, const reaction::Value& val) {
        auto var = node.lock();
        if (var) [[likely]]
            var->updateValue(val.get<int>() + 1);
    }, deltaAVar);
    batch.addOp([this](const reaction::NodePtr& node, const reaction::Value& val) {
        auto var = node.lock();
        if (var) [[likely]]
            var->updateValue(val.get<int>() + 1);
    }, deltaBVar);
    batch.execute();
    
    // 6. Record recompute count after batch
    int recomputeAfter = recomputeCountVar->getValue();
    
    // 7. Get final value
    int finalValue = sumCalc->getValue().get<int>();
    
    // 8. Return result
    return BatchExecuteResult{
        .recomputeCountBeforeBatch = recomputeBefore,
        .recomputeCountAfterBatch = recomputeAfter,
        .finalValue = finalValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <functional>

namespace test_reaction {

BatchLifecycleResult test_batch_manual_lifecycle(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    auto var = reaction::Var<int>(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create variable to track recomputation count
    int recomputeCount{0};
    
    // 3. Create calculation that increments the count when evaluated
    auto calc = reaction::Calc([this](const int& v) {
        recomputeCount++;
        return v; // Return value is ignored here
    }, var);
    
    // 4. Create Batch object
    reaction::Batch batch;
    
    // 5. Check if batch is closed initially
    bool closedInitially = batch.isClosed();
    
    // 6. Execute batch (updates var and triggers downstream calculations)
    batch.execute([&var = var](const reaction::BatchPtr& ptr) {
        var.set(updatedValue); // Update the reactive variable
    });
    
    // 7. Record recompute count after execute
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute = recomputeCount;
    
    // 8. Close the batch
    batch.close();
    
    // 9. Check if batch is closed after close
    bool closedAfterClose = batch.isClosed();
    
    // 10. Ensure calculation was evaluated (recomputeCount should be at least 1)
    bool calcEvaluated = recomputeCountAfterExecute > 0;
    
    // 11. Return the result
    return BatchLifecycleResult{
        .closedInitially = closedInitially,
        .closedAfterClose = closedAfterClose,
        .recomputeCountAfterExecute = recomputeCountAfterExecute
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <atomic>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadSafetyTransition test_thread_manager_manual() {
    reaction::ThreadManager& thread_manager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 1. Reset thread manager state
    thread_manager.reset();
    
    // 3. Register current thread
    thread_manager.registerThread(std::this_thread::get_id());
    
    // 4. Get thread count
    auto thread_count = thread_manager.getThreadCount();
    
    // 5. Get thread safety status before enabling
    auto before_enable = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 6. Manually enable thread safety
    thread_manager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // 7. Get thread safety status after enabling
    auto after_enable = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Reset thread manager state again
    thread_manager.reset();
    
    // 9. Get thread safety status after reset
    auto after_reset = thread_manager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 10. Return transition
    return ThreadSafetyTransition{
        .beforeEnable = before_enable,
        .afterEnable = after_enable,
        .afterReset = after_reset,
        .threadCount = thread_count
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <functional>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AlwaysTriggerResult {
    int recomputeCountAfterInit;
    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite;
};

AlwaysTriggerResult test_always_trigger(int initialValue) {
    VarExpr<int> var(initialValue);
    VarExpr<int> counter(0);
    
    // Create the reactive calculation with AlwaysTrig mode
    CalcExpr<int, AlwaysTrig> calc([this](const NodePtr& input) {
        counter = counter() + 1;
        return 2 * input->get();
    }, var);
    
    // Capture initial recompute count after setup
    int recomputeCountAfterInit = counter();
    
    // Reset the variable with the same value (should trigger recompute)
    var = initialValue;
    
    // Capture recompute count after duplicate write
    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite = counter();
    
    // Force evaluate the calculation to avoid unused variable warning
    int dummy = calc();
    
    return AlwaysTriggerResult{
        .recomputeCountAfterInit = recomputeCountAfterInit,
        .recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite = recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of IdTrackedFields constructor and valueField method
class IdTrackedFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    explicit IdTrackedFields(int value) : m_valueField(reaction::Var<int>(value)) {
        // Register the field to the reactive graph
        this->addObj(m_valueField.shared_from_this());
    }

    reaction::Var<int>& valueField() noexcept {
        return m_valueField;
    }

private:
    reaction::Var<int> m_valueField; ///< Reactive variable field
};

// Implementation of test_field_unique_id function
FieldIdComparison test_field_unique_id(int initialFirst, int initialSecond) {
    IdTrackedFields first(initialFirst);
    IdTrackedFields second(initialSecond);

    uint64_t firstIdFirstRead = first.getId();
    uint64_t firstIdSecondRead = first.getId();
    uint64_t secondId = second.getId();

    // Update reactive fields to avoid unused variable warnings
    int val1 = first.valueField().get();
    int val2 = second.valueField().get();

    return FieldIdComparison{
        .firstIdFirstRead = firstIdFirstRead,
        .firstIdSecondRead = firstIdSecondRead,
        .secondId = secondId
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LastHandleSnapshot {
    int initialValue;
    int afterUpdate;
    int afterSourceClose;
};

template <typename T, IsOp Op, typename Src>
using CalcExprType = reaction::CalcExpr<reaction::LastHandle, T, Src, Op>;

LastHandleSnapshot test_last_handle_restore(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    reaction::VarExpr<int> var(initialValue);
    CalcExprType<int, reaction::IdentityOp, reaction::VarExpr<int>> calc([=](const int& v) { return v; }, var);
    int initResult = calc.get();
    var.set(updatedValue);
    int updatedResult = calc.get();
    var.close();
    int closedResult = calc.get();
    return LastHandleSnapshot{initResult, updatedResult, closedResult};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResourceAtomicResult {
    int finalValue;
    bool operationReportedChange;
};

template <typename F, typename... A>
constexpr auto atomicOperation(F &&f, A &&...args) {
    return std::invoke(f = std::forward<A>(args)...);
}

ResourceAtomicResult test_resource_atomic(int initialValue, int delta) {
    reaction::Resource<int> res(initialValue);
    auto operationResult = res.atomic([delta = delta](int val) {
        return val + delta;
    });
    return ResourceAtomicResult{
        .finalValue = operationResult.first,
        .operationReportedChange = operationResult.second
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

StackMonitorReport test_stack_monitor_guard() {
    StackMonitor& sm = StackMonitor::getInstance();
    
    // 1. Disable monitoring
    sm.disable();
    
    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    sm.initialize();
    
    // 3. Create report struct
    StackMonitorReport report{};
    
    // 4. Check if monitoring is enabled before guard
    report.monitoringEnabledBefore = sm.isMonitoringEnabled();
    
    // 5. Create StackGuard in a scope block
    {
        StackGuard<sizeof(StackGuard)> guard;
        // 6. Check if guard was successfully allocated
        report.guardAllocated = !guard.isNull();
        
        // 7. Get current usage during guard
        report.usageDuringGuard = sm.getCurrentUsage();
        
        // 8. Get statistics
        StackMonitorStats stats = sm.getStats();
        report.usagePercentage = stats.usagePercentage;
        report.nearLimitDuringGuard = stats.nearLimit;
    }
    
    // 10. Exit guard scope (automatically)
    
    // 11. Get current usage after guard
    report.usageAfterGuard = sm.getCurrentUsage();
    
    // 12. Disable monitoring again
    sm.disable();
    
    // 13. Check if monitoring is enabled after disable
    report.monitoringAfterDisable = sm.isMonitoringEnabled();
    
    // 14. Return report
    return report;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    auto create_large_payload = [largeSeed](int seed) {
        LargePayload p;
        for (int i = 0; i < 64; ++i) {
            p.data[i] = seed + i;
        }
        return p;
    };

    SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallRes{initialSmall};
    SBOResource<LargePayload> largeRes{create_large_payload(largeSeed)};

    bool update_reported_change = smallRes.update(updatedSmall);

    auto small_storage = smallRes.getStorageInfo();
    auto large_storage = largeRes.getStorageInfo();

    return SboStorageStatus{
        .updateReportedChange = update_reported_change,
        .smallUsingSBO = small_storage.isUsingSBO(),
        .smallStackUsage = small_storage.stackUsage(),
        .largeInitialized = true,
        .largeUsingSBO = large_storage.isUsingSBO()};
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

        if (result.tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety) {

        }

    // Step 17: Return shared mutex result
    return result;

ResetBehaviorSnapshot test_react_reset_behavior(int initialValue, int multiplier) {

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResetBehaviorSnapshot {
    int beforeResetValue;
    int afterResetValue;
    bool safetyEnabledDuringReset;
};

ResetBehaviorSnapshot test_react_reset_behavior(int initialValue, int multiplier) {
    auto &threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    threadManager.reset();

    auto varExpr = std::make_shared<reaction::VarExpr>(initialValue);
    auto calcExpr = std::make_shared<reaction::CalcExpr>(
        [multiplier = multiplier](const reaction::NodePtr &node) {
            return node->get<int>() * multiplier;
        },
        varExpr
    );

    int beforeResetValue = calcExpr->get<int>();

    threadManager.enableSafety();
    bool safetyEnabled = threadManager.isSafetyEnabled();

    calcExpr->reset(
        [multiplier = multiplier](const reaction::NodePtr &node) {
            return node->get<int>() * multiplier;
        },
        varExpr
    );

    varExpr->set(initialValue + 1);
    int afterResetValue = calcExpr->get<int>();

    return ResetBehaviorSnapshot{
        .beforeResetValue = beforeResetValue,
        .afterResetValue = afterResetValue,
        .safetyEnabledDuringReset = safetyEnabled
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<think>Okay, I need to implement the describe_error_codes function using the reaction library. Let's see. The function takes a vector of ErrorCode enums and returns a vector of their string descriptions. 

First, the steps are: create a vector to store descriptions, reserve space for optimization, iterate through each code, convert to string, then return the vector. 

So, the reaction library probably has a way to get the string representation of an ErrorCode. Maybe there's a method like to_string() or something similar on the ErrorCode enum. Let me check the ReactionException class. Oh right, in the reaction/core/exception.h file, the ErrorCode enum is defined, and there's a static method get_error_message that converts the code to a string. So for each code in the input vector, I can call ReactionException::get_error_message(code) to get the description.

Now, the steps in code. Initialize a vector<string> descriptions. Then reserve space equal to the size of the input codes vector to avoid reallocations. Then loop through each code, append the string from get_error_message to descriptions. Finally return descriptions.

Wait, but what if the input vector is empty? Reserving zero space is okay, but maybe better to just not reserve. But the problem says to reserve space for optimization, so even if it's empty, reserving is part of the steps. So regardless of the input size, reserve the same size as the input vector.

So putting it all together:

In the function, first create the vector. Then reserve(codes.size()). Then for each code in codes, push_back the string obtained from ReactionException::get_error_message(code). Then return the vector.

Let me make sure about the namespace. The function is in the test_reaction namespace. The reaction library's ReactionException is in the reaction namespace. So when calling get_error_message, it's reaction::ReactionException::get_error_message(code).

Yes. So the code would look like:

namespace test_reaction {
std::vector<std::string> describe_error_codes(const std::vector<reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode> &codes) {

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/resource.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

ResourcePointerSnapshot test_resource_pointer_view(int initialValue, int directWriteValue) {
    // Step 1: Create Resource instance
    reaction::core::Resource<int> resource(initialValue);
    
    // Step 2: Get first raw pointer
    auto ptr1 = resource.getPtr()->get();
    
    // Step 3: Read initial pointer value
    int initialPointerValue = *ptr1;
    
    // Step 4: Write directly through pointer
    *ptr1 = directWriteValue;
    
    // Step 5: Get second raw pointer
    auto ptr2 = resource.getPtr()->get();
    
    // Step 6: Read pointer value after write
    int pointerValueAfterWrite = *ptr2;
    
    // Step 7: Check if pointer is stable
    bool pointerStable = ptr1 == ptr2;
    
    // Step 8: Return resource pointer snapshot
    return ResourcePointerSnapshot{
        .initialPointerValue = initialPointerValue,
        .pointerValueAfterWrite = pointerValueAfterWrite,
        .pointerStable = pointerStable
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator bool and operator== for validity tracking.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the reactive variable
 * @return ValiditySnapshot Snapshot describing validity/equality before and after close
 */
inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    reaction::ReactVar<int> source(initialValue);
    reaction::ReactVar<int> copyVar(source);

    bool validBefore = source.is_valid() && copyVar.is_valid();
    bool equalBefore = source.get() == copyVar.get();

    source.close();

    bool validAfterSource = source.is_valid();
    bool validAfterCopy = copyVar.is_valid();
    bool validAfter = validAfterSource && validAfterCopy;

    ValiditySnapshot snapshot{
        .firstValidBeforeClose = validBefore,
        .firstValidAfterClose = validAfter,
        .copiesEqualBeforeClose = equalBefore,
        .copiesEqualAfterClose = false,
        .equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false
    };

    try {
        snapshot.copiesEqualAfterClose = (source.get() == copyVar.get());
    } catch (const std::exception&) {
        snapshot.equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
    }

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator< for ordering reactive handles.
 *
 * @param firstName Name assigned to the first reactive node
 * @param secondName Name assigned to the second reactive node
 * @param thirdName Name assigned to the third reactive node
 * @return OrderingSnapshot Snapshot of sorted names and uniqueness flag
 */
inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Create reactive variables with provided names
    React::Var<int> var1(0, firstName);
    React::Var<int> var2(0, secondName);
    React::Var<int> var3(0, thirdName);

    // Insert into a set to order them using React's comparison operator
    std::set<React::Node *> reactSet = {&var1, &var2, &var3};

    // Collect ordered names
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    for (const auto &node : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(node->getName());
    }

    // Check if all input names are unique
    std::set<std::string> uniqueNames = {firstName, secondName, thirdName};
    bool allUnique = uniqueNames.size() == 3;

    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// Implementation of ResetAwareNode
ResetAwareNode::ResetAwareNode(std::string name) : ObserverNode(name) {}
int ResetAwareNode::notifications() const { return getNotificationCount(); }
void ResetAwareNode::clear() { setNotificationCount(0); }

// Implementation of test_graph_reset_node
ResetNodeResult test_graph_reset_node(const std::string &sourceName,
                                      const std::string &targetName) {
    auto source = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(sourceName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<ResetAwareNode>(targetName);

    auto graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph->registerNode(source);
    graph->registerNode(target);

    source->addObCb(target);

    source->notify();
    bool notifiedBeforeReset = target->notifications() > 0;

    target->clear();
    target->reset();

    source->notify();
    bool notifiedAfterReset = target->notifications() > 0;

    graph->closeNode(source);
    graph->closeNode(target);

    return ResetNodeResult{notifiedBeforeReset, notifiedAfterReset};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override { m_notifications++; }
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }
private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications{0};
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    auto owner = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("Owner");
    auto field = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("Field");
    auto &observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    auto &fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    
    observerGraph.addNode(owner);
    observerGraph.addNode(field);
    
    fieldGraph.registerField(objectId, field);
    observerGraph.addObCb(owner, field);
    
    field->valueChanged();
    bool notifiedBefore = owner->notifications() > 0;
    
    owner->clear();
    
    observerGraph.closeObservers(owner);
    fieldGraph.deleteObjFieldMap(objectId);
    
    observerGraph.addObCb(owner, field);
    field->valueChanged();
    bool notifiedAfter = owner->notifications() > 0;
    
    owner->close();
    field->close();
    
    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

StackLimitSnapshot test_stack_monitor_limits() {
    StackLimitSnapshot snapshot;

    // 1. Enable monitoring
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance().enable();
    
    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance().init();
    
    // 3. Get usage statistics before guard
    snapshot.statsBeforeGuard = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance().getUsageStats();
    
    // 4. Check if template allocation is allowed
    snapshot.templateAllocationAllowed = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance().isTemplateAllocationAllowed();
    
    // 5. Check if runtime allocation is allowed
    snapshot.runtimeAllocationAllowed = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance().isRuntimeAllocationAllowed();
    
    // 6. Create large StackGuard instance
    auto guard = reaction::memory::StackGuard::getInstance();
    snapshot.guardHeld = guard != nullptr; // Assuming getInstance() returns nullptr if failed
    
    // 7. Check if guard succeeded (already captured in guardHeld)
    
    // 8. Check if allocation blocked while guard is held
    void* ptr = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance().allocate(100);
    snapshot.allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = (ptr == nullptr);
    
    // 9. Get usage statistics after guard
    snapshot.statsAfterGuard = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance().getUsageStats();
    
    return snapshot;
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

class CountingObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot {
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial;
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate;
};

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    auto co = std::make_shared<CountingObserver>();
    auto s1 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    auto s2 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    auto s3 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();

    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.registerNode(co);
    graph.registerNode(s1);
    graph.registerNode(s2);
    graph.registerNode(s3);

    s1->addOneObserver(co);

    s1->notify();
    int firstInitial = co->notifications();

    s1->removeObserver(co);
    s2->addOneObserver(co);
    s3->addOneObserver(co);

    s1->notify();
    int firstUpdate = co->notifications();

    s2->notify();
    int secondUpdate = co->notifications();

    s3->notify();
    int thirdUpdate = co->notifications();

    graph.closeNode(co);
    graph.closeNode(s1);
    graph.closeNode(s2);
    graph.closeNode(s3);

    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        .firstNotificationsAfterInitial = firstInitial,
        .firstNotificationsAfterUpdate = firstUpdate,
        .secondNotificationsAfterUpdate = secondUpdate,
        .thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate = thirdUpdate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AtomicCompoundSnapshot {
    int afterAddition;
    int afterMultiplication;
    int afterBitwiseAnd;

output.text:
<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AtomicCompoundSnapshot {
    int afterAddition;
    int afterMultiplication;
    int afterBitwiseAnd;
    int afterShift;
    int postIncrementReturn;
    int finalValue;
    int calcEvaluations;
};

inline AtomicCompoundSnapshot test_atomic_compound_assign() {
    Var<int> var(0);
    Var<int> counter(0);
    
    Calc<int> calc([this](const VarExpr<int>& expr) {
        counter()++;
        return expr.get();
    }, VarExpr(var));
    
    var.atomicAddAssign(5);
    int afterAddition = var.get();
    
    var.atomicMultiplyAssign(3);
    int afterMultiplication = var.get();
    
    var.atomicBitwiseAndAssign(0xF);
    int afterBitwiseAnd = var.get();
    
    var.atomicLeftShiftAssign(2);
    int afterShift = var.get();
    
    int postIncrementReturn = var.atomicIncrementPost();
    int finalValue = var.get();
    
    return AtomicCompoundSnapshot{
        .afterAddition = afterAddition,
        .afterMultiplication = afterMultiplication,
        .afterBitwiseAnd = afterBitwiseAnd,
        .afterShift = afterShift,
        .postIncrementReturn = postIncrementReturn,
        .finalValue = finalValue,
        .calcEvaluations = counter.get()
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/batch.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct DepthOrderingSnapshot {
    bool initialComparatorResult;
    bool shallowBeforeDeep;
    bool deepBeforeShallow;
    bool nullComesFirst;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate ObserverNode::updateDepth cooperating with BatchCompare ordering.
 *
 * @return DepthOrderingSnapshot Comparator outcomes after adjusting node depths.
 */
inline DepthOrderingSnapshot test_observer_depth_ordering() {
    // 1. Define DepthProbeNode class
    class DepthProbeNode : public ObserverNode {
    public:
        uint16_t depth;
        DepthProbeNode() : depth(0) {}
        void updateDepth(uint16_t d) { depth = d; }
    };

    // 4. Enable thread safety
    tm.enableThreadSafety();

    shallow->updateDepth(1);

    NodeWeak deepWeak = deep;

    // 9. Update depths of nodes
    shallow->updateDepth(3);
    deep->updateDepth(4);

    // 10. Compare shallow before deep
    bool shallowBeforeDeepResult = batchCompare.compare(shallowWeak, deepWeak);

    // 11. Update depth of shallow again
    shallow->updateDepth(5);

    // 12. Compare deep before shallow
    bool deepBeforeShallowResult = batchCompare.compare(deepWeak, shallowWeak);

    // 13. Create null NodeWeak
    NodeWeak nullWeak;

    // 14. Compare null with shallow
    bool nullComesFirstResult = batchCompare.compare(nullWeak, shallowWeak);

    // 15. Return depth ordering snapshot
    return DepthOrderingSnapshot{
        .initialComparatorResult = initialResult,
        .shallowBeforeDeep = shallowBeforeDeepResult,
        .deepBeforeShallow = deepBeforeShallowResult,
        .nullComesFirst = nullComesFirstResult
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct UnaryExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    bool logicalNotAfterFlip;
    int bitwiseNotValue;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate unary expression builders and operators on reactive values.
 *
 * This function showcases how low-level expression builder helpers (`make_binary_op`,
 * `make_unary_op`) and high-level unary operators (`-`, `!`, `~`) cooperate with the
 * `expr` factory to produce reactive expressions.
 *
 * @param a First input value participating in the sum expression
 * @param b Second input value participating in the sum expression
 * @return UnaryExpressionSnapshot Snapshot of expression outputs before/after updates
 */
inline UnaryExpressionSnapshot test_unary_expression_builders(int a, int b) {
    // Step 1: Create reactive variables
    auto aVar = VarExpr::create(a);
    auto bVar = VarExpr::create(b);

    // Step 2: Build negated sum expression
    auto sumExpr = make_add_op(aVar, bVar);
    auto negSumExpr = make_unary_op(NegOp{}, sumExpr);
    auto negSumNode = createReactive(negSumExpr);

    // Step 3: Create reactive boolean variable
    auto boolVar = VarExpr::create(false);

    // Step 4: Build logical NOT expression
    auto notExpr = make_unary_op(NotOp{}, boolVar);
    auto notNode = createReactive(notExpr);

    // Step 5: Create reactive integer variable
    auto dVar = VarExpr::create(0);

    // Step 6: Build bitwise NOT expression
    auto bitNotExpr = make_unary_op(BitwiseNotOp{}, dVar);
    auto bitNotNode = createReactive(bitNotExpr);

    // Step 7: Get initial values
    int initialNegatedSum = negSumNode->get();
    bool initialLogicalNot = notNode->get();

    // Step 8: Update variables to trigger recomputation
    aVar->set(a + 1);
    bVar->set(b + 1);
    boolVar->set(!boolVar->get());
    dVar->set(5);

    // Step 9: Get updated values
    int updatedNegatedSum = negSumNode->get();
    bool updatedLogicalNot = notNode->get();
    int bitwiseNotValue = bitNotNode->get();

    // Step 10: Return snapshot
    return UnaryExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialNegatedSum = initialNegatedSum,
        .updatedNegatedSum = updatedNegatedSum,
        .logicalNotAfterFlip = updatedLogicalNot,
        .bitwiseNotValue = bitwiseNotValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

<answer>
```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LogicalExpressionSnapshot {
    bool initialAnd;
    bool initialOr;
    bool updatedAnd;
    bool updatedOr;
};

inline LogicalExpressionSnapshot test_logical_expression_builders(bool a, bool b, bool c) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto aVar = reaction::VarExpr<bool>(a);
    auto bVar = reaction::VarExpr<bool>(b);
    auto cVar = reaction::VarExpr<bool>(c); // Included per function signature, not used in expressions

    // Build logical expressions
    auto andExpr = aVar && bVar;
    auto orExpr = aVar || bVar;

    // Capture initial results
    bool initialAnd = andExpr.get();
    bool initialOr = orExpr.get();

    // Update variables to trigger recomputation
    aVar.setValue(!a);
    bVar.setValue(!b);

    // Capture updated results
    bool updatedAnd = andExpr.get();
    bool updatedOr = orExpr.get();

    return LogicalExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialAnd = initialAnd,
        .initialOr = initialOr,
        .updatedAnd = updatedAnd,
        .updatedOr = updatedOr
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/cache/cache_base.h>
#include <memory>
#include <chrono>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

struct GenericCacheStatsSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool firstLookupWasMiss;
    bool secondLookupWasHit;
};

class IntCache : public CacheBase {
public:
    using CacheBase::CacheBase; // Inherit constructors

    using CacheBase::getStats;
    using CacheBase::getHitCount;
    using CacheBase::getMissCount;
    using CacheBase::insert;
    using CacheBase::contains;
    using CacheBase::invalidateAll;
    using CacheBase::triggerCleanup;
};

GenericCacheStatsSnapshot test_generic_cache_base() {
    IntCache cache;

    const auto& initialStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesBefore = initialStats.entries;

    const int key = 42;
    const std::string value = "answer";
    bool firstWasMiss = !cache.contains(key);

    cache.insert(key, value);

    bool secondWasHit = cache.contains(key);

    const auto& postInsertStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInsert = postInsertStats.entries;
    std::size_t hitCount = postInsertStats.hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount = postInsertStats.missCount;

    cache.invalidateAll();
    cache.triggerCleanup();

    const auto& postInvalidationStats = cache.getStats();
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate = postInvalidationStats.entries;

    double hitRatio = hitCount + missCount > 0 ? static_cast<double>(hitCount) / (hitCount + missCount) : 0.0;

    return GenericCacheStatsSnapshot{
        .entriesBefore = entriesBefore,
        .entriesAfterInsert = entriesAfterInsert,
        .entriesAfterInvalidate = entriesAfterInvalidate,
        .hitCount = hitCount,
        .missCount = missCount,
        .hitRatio = hitRatio,
        .firstLookupWasMiss = firstWasMiss,
        .secondLookupWasHit = secondWasHit
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <functional>
#include <iostream>
#include <mutex>
#include <shared_mutex>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    ForceRegisterSnapshot snapshot;

    // 1. Reset thread manager state
    tm.reset();

    // 2. Check thread safety before registration
    snapshot.safetyBeforeForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    // 3. Register main thread
    tm.forceRegisterThread();

    // 4. Check thread safety after main thread registration
    snapshot.safetyAfterMainForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    // 5. Create worker thread and register it
    std::thread worker([&]() {
        auto& worker_tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
        worker_tm.forceRegisterThread();
    });
    worker.join();

    // 6. Check thread safety after worker thread registration
    snapshot.safetyAfterWorkerForce = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();

    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;
    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c) {
    // Create reactive variables
    VarExpr<int> varA(a);
    VarExpr<int> varB(b);
    VarExpr<int> varC(c);

    // Build sum expression: A + B
    auto sumExpr = make_binary_expr(varA, varB, OpAdd);

    // Build negated expression: -(A + B)
    auto negatedExpr = make_unary_expr(sumExpr, OpNeg);

    // Build chained expression: -(A + B) * C
    auto chainedExpr = make_binary_expr(negatedExpr, varC, OpMul);

    // Capture initial values
    RawExpressionSnapshot snapshot{
        .initialSum = sumExpr(),
        .initialNegatedSum = negatedExpr(),
        .initialChainedValue = chainedExpr()
    };

    // Update variables and trigger recomputation
    varA.assign(a + 1);
    varC.assign(c + 1);
    varA.notify();
    varC.notify();

    // Capture updated values
    snapshot.updatedSum = sumExpr();
    snapshot.updatedNegatedSum = negatedExpr();
    snapshot.updatedChainedValue = chainedExpr();

    return snapshot;
}

} // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/expression/atomic_operations.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * @brief Internal implementation of the atomic helper test structure.
 *
 * This class mimics the behavior of a reactive atomic variable by tracking
 * changes to its integer value and reporting whether operations modify it.
 */
class IntAtomicImpl {
public:
    /**
     * @brief Construct a new IntAtomicImpl with the given initial value.
     *
     * @param initial The starting value of the atomic variable.
     */
    explicit IntAtomicImpl(int32_t initial)
        : m_value(initial), m_lastReportedChange(false) {}

    /**
     * @brief Apply a filter operation on the stored value.
     *
     * This method executes the provided filter function on the internal value
     * and updates the change detection flag if the value changes.
     *
     * @tparam F Type of the callable filter function (expected to take no args and return void).
     * @param op A function that modifies m_value in-place.
     */
    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F &&op) {
        int32_t oldValue = m_value;
        op();
        m_lastReportedChange = (oldValue != m_value);
    }

    /**
     * @brief Apply a filter operation with forced change detection.
     *
     * This method executes the provided filter function and always reports
     * a change if forceNotify is true, overriding actual value comparison.
     *
     * @tparam F Type of the callable filter function.
     * @param op A function that modifies m_value in-place.
     * @param forceNotify If true, always consider the operation as changing the value.
     */
    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F &&op, bool forceNotify) {
        int32_t oldValue = m_value;
        op();
        if (forceNotify) {
            m_lastReportedChange = true;
        } else {
            m_lastReportedChange = (oldValue != m_value);
        }
    }

    /**
     * @brief Get the current value of the atomic variable.
     *
     * @return The current integer value.
     */
    [[nodiscard]] int32_t value() const noexcept { return m_value; }

    /**
     * @brief Check if the last operation reported a value change.
     *
     * @return True if the last operation changed the value, false otherwise.
     */
    [[nodiscard]] bool lastReportedChange() const noexcept { return m_lastReportedChange; }

private:
    std::atomic<int32_t> m_value; ///< The atomic integer value.
    std::atomic<bool> m_lastReportedChange; ///< Flag indicating if the last operation changed the value.
};

/**
 * @brief Capture the results of atomic operation tests.
 */
struct AtomicHelperSnapshot {
    int32_t finalValue;
    bool addReportedChange;
    bool multiplyReportedChange;
    bool andReportedChange;
    bool leftShiftReportedChange;
    bool incrementReportedChange;
};

/**
 * @brief Test the atomic compound assignment helper functions.
 *
 * This function demonstrates the use of reaction framework's atomic helpers
 * by applying a series of compound assignments and recording change detection.
 *
 * @param initial The starting value for the atomic variable.
 * @return A snapshot of the final state and change reports.
 */
inline AtomicHelperSnapshot test_atomic_compound_helpers(int32_t initial) {
    IntAtomicImpl impl(initial);

    // Apply each compound assignment and collect change results
    reaction::atomicAddAssign(impl, 5);
    bool addChanged = impl.lastReportedChange();

    reaction::atomicMultiplyAssign(impl, 3);
    bool multiplyChanged = impl.lastReportedChange();

    reaction::atomicBitwiseAndAssign(impl, 0xF); // 0b1111
    bool andChanged = impl.lastReportedChange();

    reaction::atomicLeftShiftAssign(impl, 1);
    bool leftShiftChanged = impl.lastReportedChange();

    reaction::atomicIncrement(impl);
    bool incrementChanged = impl.lastReportedChange();

    return AtomicHelperSnapshot{
        .finalValue = impl.value(),
        .addReportedChange = addChanged,
        .multiplyReportedChange = multiplyChanged,
        .andReportedChange = andChanged,
        .leftShiftReportedChange = leftShiftChanged,
        .incrementReportedChange = incrementChanged
    };
}

} // namespace test_reaction
